page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6r.large is an ARM64-based virtual machine instance powered by a 2.8 GHz Ampere Altra processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 2 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the KVM hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the single-core performance of the Ampere Altra processor on this instance falls within the average range, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1790.19. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance are more constrained. The stress-ng multi-core score is 3551.69, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 1945.0, both representing lower-tier performance profiles. Memory bandwidth tests show 141.08 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 24.55 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Cryptographic workloads benefit from hardware-accelerated AES support, though advanced vector extensions (AVX) and matrix extensions (AMX) are absent.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g6r.large offers dedicated ARM64 compute resources at a low entry point, making it a viable option for resource-constrained environments where shared-core performance fluctuations must be avoided. Given its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and moderate throughput capabilities—such as a static web serving throughput of 2.62 GB/sec and a Redis SET performance of 2.69 million operations per second—this instance is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads. These include basic web serving, microservices, development and testing environments, and simple utility applications that do not require high-performance multi-threaded compute or local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6r.large is an ARM64-based instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 2.8 GHz Ampere Altra processor, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the lower tiers. The instance supports hardware-accelerated AES cryptography but lacks AVX and AMX instruction sets. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.g6r.large offers a cost-efficient entry point for dedicated ARM resources. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, including basic web serving, microservices, and development environments.
